title: MCP Tools for Knowledge Write Operations

story.i_want_to: manage the knowledge wiki lifecycle through MCP tools
story.so_that: the orchestration loop can publish articles, review drafts, run lint checks, and trigger exports programmatically

rationale: The orchestrator agent manages the development loop through MCP tools rather than direct curl calls. Without MCP tools for knowledge management, the orchestrator cannot programmatically publish vetted articles, review the draft queue, run lint checks for wiki health, or trigger exports. Adding these four tools completes the knowledge intelligence loop by giving the orchestrator the same wiki management capabilities available through the REST API, following the established MCP tool patterns in mcp-server/index.js.

acceptance_criteria.description: A `knowledge_publish` MCP tool exists in mcp-server/index.js. It calls POST /api/v1/articles/:id/publish using ORCH_KEY. Accepts article_id as required parameter. Returns the published article JSON on success, or error details on failure.

A `knowledge_drafts` MCP tool exists in mcp-server/index.js. It calls GET /api/v1/knowledge/drafts using ORCH_KEY. Accepts optional limit and offset parameters. Returns the list of draft articles with source traceability.

A `knowledge_lint` MCP tool exists in mcp-server/index.js. It calls GET /api/v1/knowledge/lint (or GET /api/v1/projects/:project_id/knowledge/lint if project_id provided) using ORCH_KEY. Accepts optional project_id, stale_days, and min_coverage parameters. Returns the structured lint report.

A `knowledge_export` MCP tool exists in mcp-server/index.js. It calls GET /api/v1/knowledge/export (or GET /api/v1/projects/:project_id/knowledge/export if project_id provided) using ORCH_KEY. Returns the download URL or a message indicating the ZIP was generated. Does NOT return raw binary ZIP data.
All four tools use ORCH_KEY (not AGENT_KEY) for authentication, consistent with other management-level MCP tools.
Each tool follows the existing MCP pattern: (1) tool function defined, (2) entry added to TOOLS array with name, description, and inputSchema, (3) switch case added in the tool dispatch handler.
Tool descriptions are clear and include the expected use case. inputSchema validates required vs optional parameters with correct JSON Schema types.
Error responses from the API are surfaced as structured MCP tool errors with meaningful messages (not raw HTTP error bodies).
The tools are documented in mcp-server/README.md with usage examples, parameter descriptions, and expected responses.

test_cases.name: knowledge_publish tool publishes a draft article
preconditions: Running loopctl server with a tenant and draft article
steps: Call knowledge_publish MCP tool with article_id of a draft article
expected_results: Tool returns success with article status: "published"
Article is published in the database

test_cases.name: knowledge_drafts tool lists draft articles
preconditions: Running loopctl server with tenant having draft articles
steps: Call knowledge_drafts MCP tool with default params
expected_results: Tool returns list of draft articles
Each article includes source_type for traceability

test_cases.name: knowledge_lint tool returns structured health report
preconditions: Running loopctl server with tenant having articles in various states
steps: Call knowledge_lint MCP tool with project_id and custom thresholds
expected_results: Tool returns lint report with stale_articles, orphan_articles, contradiction_clusters, coverage_gaps, broken_sources sections

test_cases.name: knowledge_export tool triggers export
preconditions: Running loopctl server with tenant having published articles
expected_results: Tool returns success message or download URL
Does not return raw binary data

test_cases.name: Tools handle API errors gracefully
preconditions: ORCH_KEY configured but server returns 422 for invalid operation
steps: Call knowledge_publish with an already-published article_id
expected_results: Tool returns structured error message, not raw HTTP error
Error message explains the invalid status transition

technical_notes: Follow the existing MCP tool pattern in mcp-server/index.js exactly: (1) async function for each tool, (2) TOOLS array entry with name/description/inputSchema, (3) switch case in the callTool handler.
Use ORCH_KEY for all four tools since these are management operations. Pattern: `headers: { 'Authorization': `Bearer ${process.env.LOOPCTL_ORCH_KEY}` }`.

knowledge_export should NOT try to return the raw ZIP binary as MCP tool output. Instead, either save to a temp file and return the path, or return a message indicating the export endpoint URL for direct download.

inputSchema for knowledge_lint should include optional properties: project_id (string), stale_days (integer), min_coverage (integer). knowledge_publish requires article_id (string). knowledge_drafts accepts optional limit (integer) and offset (integer).
Error handling: wrap API calls in try/catch, parse error responses, and return structured `{content: [{type: 'text', text: error_message}], isError: true}` per MCP protocol.
Update mcp-server/README.md with the four new tools in the existing tools documentation section.
